claim: No Stable, release, conformance, or manual assistive-technology claim is made by this source contract.
summary: A single or multiple disclosure group with semantic headings, native triggers, controlled state, and optional roving focus keys.
semantics: Every named trigger is a native button inside a caller-selected h1-h6 and controls one disclosure panel.
Single mode rejects more than one controlled/default expanded key; all key arrays and item values are non-empty and unique where statically knowable.
Enter and Space activate natively; ArrowUp, ArrowDown, Home, and End move focus among enabled triggers without changing expansion.
Disabled items and a disabled root remain non-operable without removing their understandable names and relationships.

preferences.forcedColors: System focus and native button state remain perceivable without color-only expansion cues.
preferences.reducedMotion: Indicator rotation is removed while aria-expanded and panel visibility remain authoritative.
preferences.rtl: Logical layout mirrors while vertical navigation keys keep their physical meaning.

responsive.overflowPolicy: Labels wrap and panels reflow without clipping content or focus indicators.
